body.title: Wire tick_watch_pass() into NE cycle + watchlist GIN index
body.result: tick_watch_pass wired into NE cycle; m064 partial index for watched nodes; get_portable excludes WORD_GRAPH
body.intention: I intend that deferred nodes actually decay over time and the watchlist remains bounded.
body.description:
Expert audit (Cognitive Scientist) found tick_watch_pass() has no callers outside cortex.py — deferred nodes accumulate on watchlist indefinitely and never decay. Three fixes in one ticket: (1) wire tick_watch_pass() into the NE context cycle so watched nodes decay; (2) add GIN index on (metadata->>'watch') filtered WHERE metadata ? 'watch' to replace full-table scan across 57K+ rows; (3) exclude WORD_GRAPH type from get_portable() to prevent 57K scaffolding nodes appearing in instance-portable payload.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/cognition/narrative_engine.py (or coa.py), devices/igor/memory/cortex.py (migration for GIN index + get_portable filter)
**Design rules:** HIGH-inertia: cortex.py (pause for Akien pre-approval before coding); no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** IN — tick_watch_pass() wiring, GIN index migration, WORD_GRAPH portable exclusion; OUT — deferred node accumulation logic changes
**Completion criteria:** (1) grep confirms tick_watch_pass() is called from NE/context cycle; (2) migration adds filtered GIN index on metadata->>'watch'; (3) get_portable() excludes WORD_GRAPH type; (4) watched node count query runs <100ms on full memory table
